Functions for initializing, locking, enabling, and disabling peripheral configurations.

# Purpose
This C source code file provides a set of functions for managing peripheral configurations. It includes functions to initialize the peripheral configuration system ([`periph_config_init`](<#periph_config_init>)), acquire and release locks ([`periph_config_acquire_lock`](<#periph_config_acquire_lock>) and [`periph_config_release_lock`](<#periph_config_release_lock>)), and enable or disable peripherals ([`periph_config_enable`](<#periph_config_enable>) and [`periph_config_disable`](<#periph_config_disable>)). The functions [`periph_config_enable`](<#periph_config_enable>) and [`periph_config_disable`](<#periph_config_disable>) take a pointer to a peripheral and a 32-bit unsigned integer representing the RCC (Reset and Clock Control) bit, which is used to control the clock settings for the peripheral. The file includes the header `drivers/periph_config.h`, which likely contains necessary declarations and definitions related to peripheral configuration.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `drivers/periph_config.h`
